content.content:
variable	value	setting_type	public	description	default_value	origin
admission.disk_bandwidth_tokens.elastic.enabled	true	b	t	when true, and provisioned bandwidth for the disk corresponding to a store is configured, tokens for elastic work will be limited if disk bandwidth becomes a bottleneck	true	default
admission.elastic_cpu.adjustment_delta_per_second	0.001	f	f	sets the per-second % adjustment used when when adapting elastic work CPU %s	0.001	default
admission.elastic_cpu.enabled	true	b	f	when true, elastic work (like backups) performed by the KV layer is subject to admission control	true	default
admission.elastic_cpu.inactive_point	0.1	f	f	the point between {min,max}_utilization the CPU % decreases to when there's no elastic work	0.1	default
admission.elastic_cpu.max_utilization	0.75	f	f	sets the ceiling on per-node elastic work CPU % utilization	0.75	default
admission.elastic_cpu.min_utilization	0.05	f	f	sets the floor on per-node elastic work CPU % utilization	0.05	default
admission.elastic_cpu.multiplicative_factor_on_decrease	2	f	f	sets the multiplier on negative adjustments to elastic work CPU %	2	default
admission.elastic_cpu.multiplicative_factor_on_inactive_decrease	0.25	f	f	sets the multiplier on negative adjustments to elastic work CPU % when inactive	0.25	default
admission.elastic_cpu.scheduler_latency_target	1ms	d	f	sets the p99 scheduling latency the elastic CPU controller aims for	1ms	default
admission.epoch_lifo.enabled	false	b	t	when true, epoch-LIFO behavior is enabled when there is significant delay in admission	false	default
admission.epoch_lifo.epoch_closing_delta_duration	5ms	d	t	the delta duration before closing an epoch, for epoch-LIFO admission control ordering	5ms	default
admission.epoch_lifo.epoch_duration	100ms	d	t	the duration of an epoch, for epoch-LIFO admission control ordering	100ms	default
admission.epoch_lifo.queue_delay_threshold_to_switch_to_lifo	105ms	d	t	the queue delay encountered by a (tenant,priority) for switching to epoch-LIFO ordering	105ms	default
admission.kv.bulk_only.enabled	false	b	f	when both admission.kv.enabled and this is true, only throttle bulk work	false	default
admission.kv.enabled	true	b	t	when true, work performed by the KV layer is subject to admission control	true	default
admission.kv.pause_replication_io_threshold	0	f	f	pause replication to non-essential followers when their I/O admission control score exceeds the given threshold (zero to disable)	0	default
admission.kv.stores.tenant_weights.enabled	false	b	f	when true, tenant weights are enabled for KV-stores admission control	false	default
admission.kv.tenant_weights.enabled	false	b	f	when true, tenant weights are enabled for KV admission control	false	default
admission.kv_slot_adjuster.overload_threshold	32	i	f	when the number of runnable goroutines per CPU is greater than this threshold, the slot adjuster considers the cpu to be overloaded	32	default
admission.l0_file_count_overload_threshold	1000	i	f	when the L0 file count exceeds this theshold, the store is considered overloaded	1000	default
admission.l0_min_size_per_sub_level	5242880	i	f	when non-zero, this indicates the minimum size that is needed to count towards one sub-level	5242880	default
admission.l0_sub_level_count_overload_threshold	20	i	f	when the L0 sub-level count exceeds this threshold, the store is considered overloaded	20	default
admission.min_flush_util_fraction	0.5	f	f	when computing flush tokens, this fraction is a lower bound on the dynamically adjusted flush utilization target fraction that attempts to reduce write stalls. Set it to a high fraction (>>1, e.g. 10), to disable flush based tokens. The dynamic target fraction is used to multiply the (measured) peak flush rate, to compute the flush tokens. If the storage.write-stall-nanos indicates significant write stalls, and the granter logs show that the dynamic target fraction has already reached the lower bound, one can consider lowering it slightly (after consultation with domain experts)	0.5	default
admission.replication_control.range_sequencer_gc_threshold	5m0s	d	f	the inactive duration for a range sequencer after it's garbage collected	5m0s	default
admission.sql_kv_response.enabled	true	b	t	when true, work performed by the SQL layer when receiving a KV response is subject to admission control	true	default
admission.sql_sql_response.enabled	true	b	t	when true, work performed by the SQL layer when receiving a DistSQL response is subject to admission control	true	default
admission.wal.failover.unlimited_tokens.enabled	false	b	f	when true, during WAL failover, unlimited admission tokens are allocated	false	default
backup.restore.online_worker_count	8 B	z	f	workers to use for online restore worker phase	8 B	default
backup.restore_span.online_target_size	16 GiB	z	f	target size to which base spans of an online restore are merged to produce a restore span (0 disables)	16 GiB	default
backup.restore_span.target_size	384 MiB	z	f	target size to which base spans of a restore are merged to produce a restore span (0 disables)	384 MiB	default
backup.write_metadata_with_external_ssts.enabled	true	b	f	write BACKUP metadata along with supporting SST files	true	default
bulkio.backup.balanced_distribution.enabled	true	b	f	randomize the selection of which replica backs up each range	true	default
bulkio.backup.checkpoint_interval	1m0s	d	f	the minimum time between writing progress checkpoints during a backup	1m0s	default
bulkio.backup.deprecated_full_backup_with_subdir.enabled	false	b	t	when true, a backup command with a user specified subdirectory will create a full backup at the subdirectory if no backup already exists at that subdirectory	false	default
bulkio.backup.elide_common_prefix.enabled	true	b	f	remove common prefixes from backup file	true	default
bulkio.backup.verbose_tracing.enabled	false	b	f	send each export request with a verbose tracing span	false	default
bulkio.backup.file_size	128 MiB	z	t	target size for individual data files produced during BACKUP	128 MiB	default
bulkio.backup.file_sst_sink_elastic_control.enabled	true	b	f	determines whether the file sst sink integrates with elastic CPU control	true	default
bulkio.backup.max_workers	5	i	f	maximum number of workers to use for reading and uploading data in a backup	5	default
bulkio.backup.merge_file_buffer_size	128 MiB	z	f	size limit used when buffering backup files before merging them	128 MiB	default
bulkio.backup.presplit_request_spans.enabled	true	b	f	split the spans that will be requests before requesting them	true	default
bulkio.backup.read_retry_delay	5s	d	f	amount of time since the read-as-of time, per-prior attempt, to wait before making another attempt	5s	default
bulkio.backup.read_timeout	5m0s	d	t	amount of time after which a read attempt is considered timed out, which causes the backup to fail	5m0s	default
bulkio.backup.read_with_priority_after	1m0s	d	t	amount of time since the read-as-of time above which a BACKUP should use priority when retrying reads	1m0s	default
bulkio.backup.split_keys_on_timestamps.enabled	true	b	f	split backup data on timestamps when writing revision history	true	default
bulkio.column_backfill.batch_size	200	i	f	the number of rows updated at a time to add/remove columns	200	default
bulkio.column_backfill.update_chunk_size_threshold_bytes	10485760	i	f	the batch size in bytes above which an update is immediately run when adding/removing columns	10485760	default
bulkio.import.predicate_delete_range_batch_size	10	i	f	the number of ranges to include in a single Predicate Based DeleteRange request	10	default
bulkio.import.predicate_delete_range_parallelism	4	i	f	the number of workers used to issue Predicate Based DeleteRange request	4	default
bulkio.import.processors_per_node	1	i	f	number of input processors to run on each sql instance	1	default
bulkio.import.reader_parallelism	0	i	f	number of parallel workers to use to convert read data for formats that support parallel conversion; 0 indicates number of cores	0	default
bulkio.import.replan_flow_frequency	2m0s	d	f	frequency at which IMPORT checks to see if restarting would update its physical execution plan	2m0s	default
bulkio.import.replan_flow_threshold	0	f	f	fraction of initial flow instances that would be added or updated above which an IMPORT is restarted from its last checkpoint (0=disabled)	0	default
bulkio.import.retry_duration	2m0s	d	f	duration during which the IMPORT can be retried in face of non-permanent errors	2m0s	default
bulkio.import.write_import_epoch.enabled	true	b	f	controls whether IMPORT will write ImportEpoch's to descriptors	true	default
bulkio.index_backfill.batch_size	50000	i	f	the number of rows for which we construct index entries in a single batch	50000	default

Set it to a high fraction (>>1, e.g. 10), to disable flush based tokens. The dynamic target fraction is used to multiply the (measured) peak flush rate, to compute the flush tokens. If the storage.write-stall-nanos indicates significant write stalls, and the granter logs show that the dynamic target fraction has already reached the lower bound, one can consider lowering it slightly (after consultation with domain experts)	0.5	default
